acceptedQuestions.subject: Environment
contextLines: How many of the above are traditionally known as camel-rearing pastoralist communities in India?
acceptedQuestions.explanation: All four communities listed—Raika, Rabari, Fakirani Jat, and Manganiyar—are traditional pastoralist communities in India known for camel-rearing, primarily in the dryland ecosystems of Rajasthan and Gujarat.
acceptedQuestions.takeaway: The Raika, Rabari, Fakirani Jat, and Manganiyar are key pastoralist communities historically associated with camel rearing in India's dryland ecosystems.
acceptedQuestions.evidenceExcerpt: Camel-rearing Pastoralist communities: Raika, Rabari, Fakirani Jat, and Manganiyar...
acceptedQuestions.reviewNotes: Rewrote the candidate question to fit the 'How many of the above' format, which aligns perfectly with recent UPSC Prelims trends for questions on traditional communities.

reviewerNotes: Selected and refined three distinct questions from the source text. Q1 focuses on traditional pastoralist communities using the modern 'How many of the above' format. Q2 covers population trends, geography, and policy initiatives. Q3 tests conceptual knowledge of biological adaptations, specifically targeting a common misconception. All questions are strictly grounded in the provided text and adhere to UPSC standards.
